anxiety.label: Anxiety & Worry
anxiety.summary: What you’re describing sounds like anxiety — that persistent hum of worry that’s hard to turn off. It’s one of the most common mental health experiences and it’s very treatable.

free_resource.title: Understanding Anxiety: A Plain-Language Guide
free_resource.description: Learn what anxiety actually is, why it happens, and 5 research-backed ways to reduce it this week.

upsell.title: Calm Your Nervous System in 7 Days

depression.label: Low Mood & Depression
depression.summary: It sounds like you’ve been dealing with low mood — that heavy, flat feeling that makes everything harder. This is a very real experience and there are things that genuinely help.

free_resource.title: When Everything Feels Heavy: Understanding Low Mood
free_resource.description: A compassionate guide to understanding depression and the small steps that actually make a difference.

upsell.title: Lifting the Weight: A Depression Workbook

relationships.label: Relationships & Loneliness
relationships.summary: Relationship struggles and loneliness hit deep — we’re wired for connection. What you’re feeling makes complete sense.

free_resource.title: Connection 101: Understanding Why Relationships Are Hard
free_resource.description: Why relationships take work and what the research says about keeping them healthy.

grief.label: Grief & Loss
grief.summary: Grief is one of the most disorienting experiences a person can go through. There’s no timeline and no right way to do it.

free_resource.title: Understanding Grief: You’re Not Going Crazy
free_resource.description: A compassionate guide to grief, its stages, and what tends to actually help.

work.label: Work & Life Stress
work.summary: Work stress and burnout are real and serious. What you’re carrying is heavy — and often invisible to the people around you.

free_resource.title: Burnout vs. Stress: What’s Actually Happening to You
free_resource.description: Understanding burnout and the evidence-based steps that help you recover.

trauma.label: Trauma & Difficult Memories
trauma.summary: Trauma leaves a mark — in your body, your thoughts, and your reactions. It’s not weakness. It’s a normal response to abnormal experiences.

free_resource.title: Understanding Trauma: Why Your Brain Is Protecting You
free_resource.description: A plain-language guide to trauma responses, triggers, and what evidence says actually helps.

recovery.label: Substance Use & Recovery
recovery.summary: Reaching this point — even just reflecting on it — takes courage. Substance use is complex, and so is recovery. You don’t have to figure it out alone.

free_resource.title: Understanding Substance Use: It’s Not a Moral Failure
free_resource.description: How addiction works in the brain and what recovery actually looks like.

family.label: Family Stress
family.summary: Family stress can be all-consuming, especially because you can’t always just walk away. What you’re feeling is valid.

free_resource.title: Navigating Difficult Family Relationships
free_resource.description: Understanding family dynamics and protecting your own wellbeing.

loneliness.label: Loneliness & Isolation
loneliness.summary: Loneliness is one of the most painful human experiences — and one of the most common. You’re far from alone in feeling this way.

free_resource.title: The Loneliness Trap: Understanding and Escaping It
free_resource.description: Why loneliness is so hard to shake and what actually helps build real connection.

general.label: General Wellbeing
general.summary: It sounds like you’ve got a lot going on. It can be hard to name one thing — sometimes it all builds up at once.

free_resource.title: Mental Health 101: Understanding What You’re Going Through
free_resource.description: A foundational guide to mental health, common struggles, and where to start.

low_risk.label: Low Risk
low_risk.summary: Your responses suggest you’re doing fairly well overall. Nothing here looks urgent right now, and you’ve got solid momentum to build on.
low_risk.next_step: Continue the habits that are working. If anything shifts, come back and check in.
low_risk.disclaimer: This is a psychoeducational tool, not a clinical assessment. It cannot diagnose any condition. Please speak with a licensed mental health professional if you have concerns about your mental health.

moderate_risk.label: Moderate Concern
moderate_risk.summary: Your responses suggest you’re carrying a meaningful amount of stress or difficulty. It's affecting you, and it’s worth taking seriously.
moderate_risk.next_step: Consider talking to a therapist or counselor — even a few sessions can make a significant difference. In the meantime, the resources below are a good starting point.
moderate_risk.disclaimer: This is a psychoeducational tool, not a clinical assessment. It cannot diagnose any condition. Please speak with a licensed mental health professional if you have concerns about your mental health.

high_risk.label: High Concern
high_risk.summary: Your responses suggest you’re really struggling right now. This level of difficulty warrants real support, not just self-help resources.
high_risk.next_step: Please reach out to a mental health professional as soon as possible. If cost or access is a barrier, Psychology Today’s directory (psychologytoday.com/therapists) has filters for sliding-scale therapists.
high_risk.disclaimer: This is a psychoeducational tool, not a clinical assessment. It cannot diagnose any condition. Please speak with a licensed mental health professional if you have concerns about your mental health.

critical.label: Critical — Please Reach Out
critical.summary: Some of your responses indicate you may be in crisis or very close to it. Please reach out to someone right now.
critical.next_step: Call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) — available 24/7. You can also text HOME to 741741 to reach the Crisis Text Line. If you’re in immediate danger, call 911.
critical.disclaimer: This is a psychoeducational tool, not a clinical assessment. It cannot diagnose any condition. Please speak with a licensed mental health professional if you have concerns about your mental health.
